title: [lichen_l2.c:632-633] net_pkt_unref after successful send assumes pkt is valid
description: In lichen_l2_send() at line 632-633, net_pkt_unref(pkt) is called after successful TX to transfer ownership per the L2 contract. However, if the net_pkt was corrupted during the send path (e.g., by a buggy LoRa driver modifying the frags chain), this unref could crash. While this is unlikely given Zephyr's net_pkt API guarantees, a defensive check like 'if (pkt != NULL)' before unref would prevent null deref if a future refactor introduces a bug where pkt is set to NULL in the error path. Low priority since current code is correct.
close_reason: Not a bug: packet is guaranteed valid because lichen_lora_l2_tx() only receives static buffer data, not the net_pkt. Added clarifying comment explaining ownership semantics.
